title: John Boscariol and Robert Glasgow Publish Anti-corruption Q&As with Practical Law’s Cross-border Resource Centre
content:
## John Boscariol and Robert Glasgow Publish Anti-corruption Q&As with Practical Law’s Cross-border Resource Centre
As corruption continues to be an increasingly important international issue, it’s vital that companies have effective safeguards in place to prevent financial and reputational damage.
John Boscariol, head of the McCarthy Tétrault’s International Trade & Investment Law Group, and Robert Glasgow senior associate in the firm’s International Trade & Investment Law Group contributed two anti-corruption Q&As to Practical Law’s Cross-border Resource Centre.
Each publication provides vital information on the laws and policies against bribery and corruption within the Canadian context:
Anti-corruption (joint-ventures) Q&A: Canada
Anti-corruption (private company acquisitions) Q&A: Canada
Compliance with international trade and investment regulations is critical to business success. McCarthy Tétrault’s International Trade & Investment Law Group advise clients in navigating the seemingly impenetrable maze presented by these regulations. To read more about our International Trade & Investment Law Group, click here .

#### About McCarthy Tétrault
McCarthy Tétrault LLP provides a broad range of legal services, advising on large and complex assignments for Canadian and international interests. The firm has substantial presence in Canada’s major commercial centres as well as in New York City and London, UK.
Built on an integrated approach to the practice of law and delivery of innovative client services, the firm brings its legal talent, industry insight and practice experience to help clients achieve the results that are important to them.
